title: OSMM Journey Strategy Object
description: The orchestrated path a customer travels: the journey goal, the stages a customer moves through, the triggers that advance them, and the sequencing/cadence logic. A journey may serve a single campaign or run always-on as a lifecycle journey independent of any campaign, so its link to a Campaign Strategy is optional. It does not define campaign scope/channel/offer mapping (that is Campaign Strategy) and is distinct from its later operational implementation (Journey Configuration, Phase 6). Canonical schema for object_type 'journey_strategy'. The osmm-journey-strategy-builder skill references this file; it is the single source of truth for the object's shape.

osmm_version.description: OSMM schema version this instance conforms to (semantic version).

journey_strategy_id.description: Stable, human-readable id. Prefix 'JNS-' + lowercase hyphen slug, scoped to the entity plus journey. Never changes once assigned.

version.description: Instance version (e.g. '1.0'). Bump on revision; the id stays stable.

name.description: A readable label for the journey (e.g. "Wendy's App First-Order to Habitual-Use Journey").

journey_goal.description: The outcome the journey drives — the single result moving a customer through this path is meant to produce (sub-process 4.2).

journey_type.description: Optional. Governed vocabulary classifying the journey's dominant motion. Pick the single closest; use 'lifecycle' for an always-on journey spanning several motions.

stages.description: The customer path — the ordered stages a customer moves through (sub-process 4.2). Order in the array is the journey order.

objective.description: What this stage is trying to achieve for the customer / for the journey.

touchpoints.description: Optional. The interactions/messages that occur in this stage (e.g. 'Welcome email', 'In-app prompt').

entry_criteria.description: Optional. What moves a customer into this stage.

exit_criteria.description: Optional. What moves a customer out of this stage (typically into the next).

triggers.description: The triggering logic — the events that advance a customer and the response each fires (sub-process 4.5).

trigger.description: The event or condition that fires (e.g. 'App download', 'First order placed', '14 days without an order').

action.description: The response the trigger fires (e.g. 'Send onboarding offer', 'Advance to retention stage').

timing.description: Optional. When the action fires relative to the trigger (e.g. 'Immediately', 'After 24h', 'Next business day').

sequencing_logic.description: Optional. How stages and messages are sequenced over the journey — ordering, branching, and overall pacing (sub-process 4.5).

cadence.description: Optional. Frequency/pacing guidance for contact across the journey (e.g. 'No more than 2 messages/week; suppress for 48h after a purchase').

channels.description: Optional. The touchpoint channels used across the journey (e.g. 'Email', 'Push', 'SMS', 'Paid social').

entry_criteria.description: Optional. Who or what enters the journey — the qualifying conditions for journey entry overall.

exit_criteria.description: Optional. Who or what exits the journey — completion, disqualification, or opt-out conditions.

linked_campaign_strategy.description: Optional. Id of the Campaign Strategy this journey serves, if it is tied to one. Omit for an always-on lifecycle journey. Use 'CMS-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' if expected but unbuilt.

linked_audiences.description: Optional. Ids of the Audience Objects this journey serves. References, not restated definitions. Use 'AUD-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' for an unbuilt audience.

linked_personas.description: Optional. Ids of the Persona Objects this journey is designed around. Use 'PER-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' for an unbuilt persona.

linked_business_context.description: Optional. Id of the owning Business Context Object. Use 'BIZ-PLACEHOLDER-<slug>' until it exists.

source.description: One line: what source(s) this was built from and approximate date.
